genres.label: Steampunk-Victorian
false_friends: Engineer, inventor, or artificer is an occupation, not automatic device ownership.
Brass, gears, goggles, and corsets are aesthetic evidence, not capabilities.
An airship does not grant personal flight.
Clockwork does not imply an armed automaton.

genres.label: Dieselpunk-World War
false_friends: Fire support is not elemental fire.
Tank crew does not imply personal ownership of a tank.
A gas mask or chemical resistance does not grant a gas weapon.
Suppressive fire is positional control, not merely damage.
Battery may mean an artillery unit rather than stored charge.

genres.label: Crime-Noir
false_friends: Narrated evidence is not automatically collected evidence.
Heat is police attention here, not thermal energy.
Mark may mean a victim of a con rather than a combat status.
Muscle may mean an enforcer rather than anatomy.
A contact is a social relation, not a summoned ally.

genres.label: Western-Frontier
false_friends: Wanted is a legal status, not desire.
Bounty hunter does not grant a supernatural quarry mark.
Frontier identity does not grant survival mastery.
Horsepower is not an animal.
Indigenous identity must never grant stereotyped skills or mysticism.

genres.label: Pirate-Nautical
false_friends: Captain is a title, not automatic sailing or command capability.
Privateer is legal status, not a skill package.
Hook hand and peg leg may be cosmetic or prosthetic rather than weapons.
Boarding means entering a vessel; rigging may mean ropes or cheating.
A sinking feeling is emotional unless vessel state is explicit.

genres.label: Samurai-Feudal Japan
false_friends: Samurai rank does not grant sword mastery, honor immunity, or supernatural ki.
Ninja is not a synonym for samurai.
Daimyo and shogun are titles, not automatic command powers.
Sword possession does not grant every sword school.
Spirit may mean morale, temperament, ancestor, or supernatural entity.

genres.label: Military-War
false_friends: Soldier, medic, engineer, commander, and sniper labels do not grant equipment or authority.
Cover and concealment are distinct.
Suppressed may describe pinned personnel or a disabled weapon.
Fire support is not fire magic; command is not mind control.
A radio call does not prove artillery availability.

genres.label: Superhero-Modern
false_friends: Hero, villain, vigilante, mutant, or alien labels do not grant powers.
A power descriptor such as cosmic, fire, divine, or technological does not define its effect.
Cape and costume do not grant flight or armor.
Invulnerable may be rhetoric rather than complete immunity.
A visual duplicate is not necessarily an authorized summon.

genres.label: Alternate History
false_friends: Alternate-history genre does not grant time travel, portals, future knowledge, or advanced weapons.
An anachronism may be ordinary local technology.
Counterfactual, parallel world, time loop, and secret history are distinct.
A hypothetical what-if statement is not a committed world mutation.
Familiar historical names do not authorize imported biography.

genres.label: School-Academy
false_friends: Class may mean a course, scheduled session, social class, or RPG class.
Grade may mean an assessment, year level, rank, or material quality.
Club may mean a student organization or a weapon.
A student of magic does not know every spell.
Detention is institutional discipline, not physical restraint unless explicitly established.
